workflow.critical: The workflow execution engine is governed by: {project-root}/_bmad/core/tasks/workflow.xml
You MUST have already loaded and processed: {installed_path}/workflow.yaml

MANDATORY UX GUARDIAN CHECK: Khi Assembly & Gap Check UI, băt buộc phải tuân theo `{project-root}/.agent/skills/ux-guardian/SKILL.md` và tham chiếu Behavior token tại `ux-patterns.yaml`. Nếu phát sinh Pattern mới, hãy thực hiện cắm cờ [NEW_UX_PATTERN_PROPOSAL].

step.action: Read the UI Spec file for the current story
Extract ALL screens/views described in the UI Spec
Load _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/design-system/stitch-project-registry.json to identify the canonical Stitch project for the target portal
Call mcp_StitchMCP_list_screens(projectId) to get all screens in the Stitch project
Match each UI Spec screen to a Stitch screen
Assign Tiers: Tier 1 (Direct match), Tier 2 (Shared match), Tier 3 (No match)

check.critical: NO SCREEN MAY PROCEED TO CODE WITHOUT A STITCH-APPROVED VISUAL
check.action: For each Tier 3 screen: Build a detailed prompt describing the screen
Call mcp_StitchMCP_generate_screen_from_text to generate the missing screen on Stitch
Present the generated screen to the user for review
check.ask: Approve / Edit / Regenerate the newly created Stitch screen?
check.action: Update Screen Inventory to Tier 1 once approved

step.action: For each Tier 1 screen: Call mcp_StitchMCP_get_screen
Extract HTML structure, CSS styles, and component patterns from the response
Save extracted code to a temporary reference file in /tmp/stitch-extract/{screenId}.html
Build a Component Registry documenting all usable structural components and patterns

check.if: MCP connection fails or times out
check.action: Implement backoff and retry. Prompts user if permanent failure

step.action: Transform ALL hardcoded Stitch CSS colors and measurements into CSS Variable tokens (e.g., var(--admin-primary))
Load and use the Token Mapping Reference from _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/stitch-first-playbook.md for converting values
Replace generic Stitch classes with arbitrary/utility values following project design rules
Map font definitions to var(--font-sans) or var(--font-mono)

check.if: unmapped or unrecognized CSS value is encountered
check.action: Map to nearest semantic token and log a warning in the output documentation

step.action: Assemble the adapted components into the final page/component layout
Add React state, hooks, API connections as required
Run grep regex check targeting hardcoded colors (e.g. bg-[#...], text-[#...]) in the generated code

check.action: FAIL the check, find matching CSS variables, and replace

step.action: Run local Vegeta server if not already running
Capture a browser screenshot of the newly assembled UI using browser_subagent
Compare the screenshot against the original Stitch output
Format all extracted details, mappings, and outputs into {story-key}-stitch-extract.md
Save the extraction reference document into {implementation_artifacts}/
